lede: A look at December 29 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Ethan Mbappé and Wang Ji-hye, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Jeju Air Flight 2216
events.desc: Jeju Air Flight 2216 crashes into a wall in Muan, South Korea, killing 179 of the 181 occupants. It is the worst aircraft accident on South Korean soil in history.

events.title: South Africa
events.desc: South Africa files a genocide case against Israel at the International Court of Justice over Israeli conducts in the Gaza Strip.

events.title: Khmer Rouge
events.desc: Leaders of the Khmer Rouge apologize for the Cambodian genocide that claimed over one million lives.

events.title: Turkish Airlines Flight 278
events.desc: Turkish Airlines Flight 278 (a Boeing 737-400) crashes on approach to Van Ferit Melen Airport in Van, Turkey, killing 57 of the 76 people on board.

events.title: 1975 LaGuardia Airport bombing
events.desc: A bomb explodes at LaGuardia Airport in New York City, killing 11 people and injuring more than 75.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: in the Second Great Fire of London, the Luftwaffe fire-bombs London, England, killing almost 200 civilians.

events.title: Washington Naval Treaty
events.desc: Japan renounces the Washington Naval Treaty of 1922 and the London Naval Treaty of 1930.

events.title: Cecil B. DeMille
events.desc: Cecil B. DeMille starts filming Hollywood's first feature film, The Squaw Man.

events.title: Ashtabula River railroad disaster
events.desc: The Ashtabula River railroad disaster occurs, leaving 64 injured and 92 dead at Ashtabula, Ohio.

events.title: Texas annexation
events.desc: The United States annexes the Republic of Texas and admits it as the 28th state.

deaths.text: Benjamin Curtis (musician), American guitarist, drummer, and songwriter
Basil William Robinson, British art scholar and author
Harold Macmillan, English captain and politician, Prime Minister of the United Kingdom
Beryl Rubinstein, American pianist, composer and teacher
